title: PaymentDispute
description: Per-payment dispute lifecycle. Tenant-visible pre-chargeback lifecycle with evidence submission window. May escalate to Chargeback via the chargeback module. Carries `tenant_id` only. NEVER carries `application_id`, `region_id`, `organization_id`, `branch_id`, or `scope_node_id`.

payment_id.description: The disputed payment. RESTRICT — payments with open disputes cannot be hard-deleted.

provider.description: Enum matching the payment's provider. Immutable after create.

provider_reference_id.description: ENCRYPTED AT REST. Provider dispute identifier: dp_... (Stripe), dispute_id (Adyen), etc. Immutable after create.

reason.description: Enum: fraudulent / duplicate / product_not_received / product_unacceptable / subscription_canceled / credit_not_processed / general / customer_initiated / unrecognized. Frozen at open. Regulator + provider reporting.

status.description: Enum: warning_needs_response / warning_under_review / warning_closed / needs_response / under_review / charge_refunded / won / lost. Warning states = provider early-warning (funds not yet withdrawn). Full states = funds provisionally withdrawn.

amount_cents.description: The disputed amount (in cents). May equal or be less than payment.amount_cents (partial disputes allowed).

evidence_due_by.description: Provider-communicated deadline for evidence submission. Nullable for warning states + terminal states. NotifyDisputeEvidenceDueJob fires 3 days before.

evidence_submitted_at.description: When we called the provider's evidence-submit API. Set once; IMMUTABLE thereafter (a resubmit creates a new dispute in some providers).

evidence.description: ENCRYPTED AT REST. Shape: { customer_signature?, receipt?, service_documentation?, product_description?, refund_policy?, communication_log?, uncategorized_text? }. Populated by CollectDisputeEvidenceJob at dispute open. Fields correspond to Stripe's evidence-file shape (widely adopted).

opened_at.description: When the dispute was opened by the provider (from webhook). Set once; IMMUTABLE.

closed_at.description: When the dispute reached a terminal status. Set once; IMMUTABLE.

outcome.description: Enum: won / lost / accepted / warning_closed_without_dispute. NULL until status transitions to terminal. Set once; IMMUTABLE.

outcome_reason.description: Provider-communicated outcome reason. Truncated to 256 chars.

refund_id.description: If ops chose to refund rather than fight (POST /accept), the refund reference. NULL when the dispute was fought or lost without refund.

chargeback_id.description: If the dispute escalated (status='lost' → EscalateDisputeToChargebackJob creates Chargeback row via chargeback module), the chargeback reference. NULL for won/accepted/warning_closed.

x-invariants: state transitions: warning_needs_response → {warning_under_review, warning_closed, needs_response}; warning_under_review → {warning_closed, needs_response}; warning_closed is TERMINAL; needs_response → {under_review, charge_refunded}; under_review → {won, lost}; charge_refunded / won / lost are TERMINAL
amount_cents <= payment.amount_cents when payment.currency = dispute.currency (partial disputes allowed; full-amount is common)
closed_at set when status transitions to a terminal state; immutable after set
outcome required when status IN ('won', 'lost', 'charge_refunded', 'warning_closed'); NULL otherwise
evidence_submitted_at set at most once — provider re-submission behaviour varies; module treats submit as one-shot
refund_id set only when outcome='accepted' (status='charge_refunded' via POST /accept path)
chargeback_id set only when outcome='lost' AND EscalateDisputeToChargebackJob completed
reason immutable after create — regulator + provider reporting depends on frozen value
hard-deletion FORBIDDEN — disputes retain 7 years (regulator audit; 10y Enterprise)
TenantErased cascade via FK CASCADE (subject to financial-record archival exception)
